title: LGBTQ2+ Advocacy Groups - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > LGBTQ2+ Advocacy Groups 6 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BASIC RIGHTS OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Builds and supports political policies that promote equality for lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgender Oregonians. Provides various services including information and referral to legal services, youth mentorship programs, education and outreach to the business community, political advocacy, and offers volunteer opportunities. Eligibility: Individuals with interest in LGBTQ2+ issues Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 222-6151 Visit Website More Details MID-WILLAMETTE TRANSGENDER SUPPORT NETWORK CASE MANAGEMENT AND RESOURCE DISPATCHING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocacy and resource navigation including finding gender-affirming healthcare providers, food, housing, medical supplies and obtaining identification documents. 260 SW Madison Avenue Suite 110 Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Individuals who are queer, transgender, non-binary, intersex, gender non-conforming, or intersex and their parents/guardians or close relatives or kinship Hours: Sunday-Friday noon-5pm or by appointment Email (541) 264-1973 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIONAL CENTER FOR LGBTQ RIGHTS LEGAL INFORMATION LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance on civil and human rights issues affecting LGBTQ2+ individuals, including public policy advocacy, impact litigation, legal help, and education on legal rights. Specific online forms for custody disputes, separation or divorce, immigration or asylum, and other issues. 870 Market Street Suite 370 San Francisco, CA 94102 Eligibility: LGBTQ2+ individuals and their families Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (800) 528-6257 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details PFLAG LGBTQ2+ SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about support groups and specialized information and referral through local chapters of the organization. 1625 K Street NW Suite 700 Washington, DC 200065112 Eligibility: Individuals who identify as LGBTQ2+ and their friends and families Hours: Not applicable Email (202) 467-8180 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE HARBOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a day center, domestic and sexual violence advocacy, culturally specific LGBTQ2+ and Latinx services, relocation assistance, transportation to a safe location, accompaniment to medical or forensic exams, assistance applying for Crime Victims Compensation, and information and referral to community agencies. Provides educational programs. These programs focus on raising awareness about the dynamics of domestic and sexual violence, understanding trauma, and promoting prevention strategies within the community. Currently accepting volunteers. 801 Commercial Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Age 14 and older Hours: Advocacy Office: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-4pm, Wednesday 9am-noon, closed 1st Wednesday of every month. Online Chat and Text: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (503) 325-5735 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITE OREGON UNITE OREGON ROGUE VALLEY HATE CRIME REPORTING AND ADVOCACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Coordinates incident reporting of planned or committed acts of violence or intimidation against people on the basis of their race, ethnic origin, nationality, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, or other stereotyped characteristic. Provides advocacy, support, and assistance to victims of hate crimes and/or witnesses to reporting an issue that occurred. 1390 SE 122nd Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 772-4029 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-WILLAMETTE TRANSGENDER SUPPORT NETWORK CASE MANAGEMENT AND RESOURCE DISPATCHING THE HARBOR THE HARBOR UNITE OREGON HATE CRIME REPORTING AND ADVOCACY
